name: Financial Success Frameworks
description: Wealth Pyramid, Profit First, and Cashflow Quadrant for financial strategy and discipline.
when_to_use: User has financial goals or business revenue questions
Task involves budgeting, investment, or financial planning
User wants to build a business or transition income sources
Agent is analyzing financial strategies or business models

wealth_pyramid.name: The Wealth Pyramid
wealth_pyramid.description: Structured financial growth from foundation upward. Each level must be solid before building the next.
wealth_pyramid.best_for: Personal finance planning, prioritizing financial actions, identifying gaps

levels.name: Foundation — Protection
levels.question: Is the financial foundation solid? Emergency fund? Insurance?
levels.priority: Build this FIRST before any investing

levels.name: Security — Debt Elimination
levels.question: Is high-interest debt cleared? Is there a budgeting system?
levels.priority: No investing until high-interest debt is gone

levels.name: Accumulation — Investing
levels.question: Are tax-advantaged retirement accounts being maximized?

levels.name: Growth — Asset Building
levels.question: Are assets generating income beyond a paycheck?
levels.priority: Build assets that produce cash flow

levels.name: Preservation — Wealth Protection
levels.question: Is accumulated wealth protected and tax-optimized?
levels.priority: Shift from growth to preservation and legacy

profit_first.name: Profit First (Mike Michalowicz)
profit_first.description: Allocate profit first, then operate on what remains. A behavioral system for financial discipline.
profit_first.best_for: Business owners, freelancers, anyone whose income requires active financial discipline
profit_first.core_formula: Sales - Profit = Expenses (NOT Sales - Expenses = Profit)

allocation_accounts.profit: Allocate percentage immediately (start with 1%, grow to 5-20%)
allocation_accounts.owners_pay: Your salary — what you pay yourself consistently

implementation_steps: Open separate bank accounts for each allocation
On the 10th and 25th of each month, allocate from Income to other accounts
Start with small profit percentage and increase by 1% each quarter
Operating expenses must fit within what remains — this forces efficiency
Take quarterly profit distributions as a reward
profit_first.key_principle: Parkinson's Law applied to money: expenses expand to fill available funds. By removing profit first, you force efficiency in spending.

cashflow_quadrant.name: Cashflow Quadrant (Robert Kiyosaki)
cashflow_quadrant.description: Four ways to earn income, each with different mindsets and financial outcomes.
cashflow_quadrant.best_for: Career and income strategy, understanding wealth-building paths, planning transitions

E_employee.description: Trades time for money. Values security and benefits.
E_employee.mindset: I need a good job with a steady paycheck
E_employee.path_forward: Build skills and savings to fund transition to B or I

S_self_employed.description: Owns a job. Works for themselves but IS the business.
S_self_employed.mindset: If I want it done right, I'll do it myself
S_self_employed.income_type: Linear — still trading time, but with more control
S_self_employed.path_forward: Systematize and delegate to transition from S to B

B_business_owner.description: Owns a system that works without them. Leverages other people's time.
B_business_owner.mindset: I build systems and hire great people
B_business_owner.income_type: Scalable — income can grow beyond personal time investment
B_business_owner.path_forward: Use business profits to fund investments (I quadrant)

I_investor.description: Money works for them. Leverages other people's money and systems.
I_investor.mindset: I put money to work and it generates returns
I_investor.income_type: Passive — income generated by assets, not time
I_investor.path_forward: Continuously acquire and optimize income-producing assets

transition_questions: Which quadrant are you primarily in right now?
Which quadrant do you want to be in?
What skills, systems, or capital do you need for that transition?
What's the smallest step to begin moving to the right side?
